content:
[S1]: Inside your body, tiny parts called cells need special instructions to know how to work and grow.  
[READER]: Hmm, I know cells are small, but what kind of instructions? This sounds interesting, tell me more!  

[S2]: These instructions come from something called DNA, which is like a big recipe book inside the cell.  
[READER]: Oh, a recipe book sounds cool! But how does the cell use that big book?  

[S3]: Since the DNA is too big to leave the cell’s control center, it makes a copy of just one recipe using something called mRNA.  
[READER]: Okay, so mRNA is like a copy? I want to know what happens to the copy next!  

[S4]: The mRNA is like a little messenger that carries the recipe out of the control center and into the main part of the cell.  
[READER]: A messenger! That makes sense. But what does the messenger do with the recipe?  

[S5]: Once the mRNA messenger arrives, the cell uses the recipe to build special things called proteins that help the cell do its job.  
[READER]: Proteins sound important! How does the cell know how to build them?  

[S6]: The mRNA has a special code made of letters that tells the cell exactly which pieces to put together to make the protein.  
[READER]: Letters as a code? Like a secret message? That’s really cool!  

[S7]: So, mRNA is very important because it carries the instructions from the big DNA book to the part of the cell that makes proteins.  
[READER]: Now I get it! The mRNA is like a helpful messenger bringing instructions so the cell knows what to do.  

[S8]: Without mRNA, the cell wouldn’t know how to make the proteins it needs to keep you healthy and strong!  
[READER]: Wow, mRNA is super important! I want to learn more about how cells work now!  

===BEGIN===  
Inside your body, tiny parts called cells need special instructions to know how to work and grow.  
These instructions come from something called DNA, which is like a big recipe book inside the cell.  
Since the DNA is too big to leave the cell’s control center, it makes a copy of just one recipe using something called mRNA.  
The mRNA is like a little messenger that carries the recipe out of the control center and into the main part of the cell.  
Once the mRNA messenger arrives, the cell uses the recipe to build special things called proteins that help the cell do its job.  
The mRNA has a special code made of letters that tells the cell exactly which pieces to put together to make the protein.  
So, mRNA is very important because it carries the instructions from the big DNA book to the part of the cell that makes proteins.  
Without mRNA, the cell wouldn’t know how to make the proteins it needs to keep you healthy and strong!  
===END===
